title: Standard tool macro migration — ToolArgs derive and follow-up wiring for all 16 standard tools
purpose: Migrate all 16 standard Norn tools to use the #[derive(ToolArgs)] macro for input schema generation and the tool_follow_ups! macro for output-to-follow-on action registration. Currently 3/16 tools use ToolArgs (bash, edit, apply_patch) and 0/16 use tool_follow_ups!. This ensures consistency across the tool surface and establishes the pattern that namespace tools (NR-006, NR-007) are mandated to follow.
task: For each of the 16 standard tools registered in register_standard_tools (registry_builder.rs), add #[derive(ToolArgs)] to the input parameter struct (if not already present) and wire a tool_follow_ups! invocation with output-to-follow-on mappings sourced from docs/design/norn-tools/tool-overview.md. The ToolArgs derive generates input_schema from struct field types and doc comments — replace manual serde_json::json!({}) schema implementations with XxxArgs::json_schema(). The tool_follow_ups! macro generates a follow-up registration closure from declarative action definitions — each action has a name, description, when condition (checked against &ToolOutput), expiry, and optional overrides. Reference the existing BashTool (crates/norn/src/tools/bash.rs) as the ToolArgs pattern exemplar.
dependency_setup: No new cargo dependencies. ToolArgs and tool_follow_ups! are re-exported from norn::tool (source: norn-macros crate). All tools are in crates/norn/src/tools/.

requirements.title: ToolArgs derive for 13 remaining tools
requirements.spec: For each tool listed below that does NOT already have #[derive(ToolArgs)] on its input args struct, THE SYSTEM SHALL: (1) add `use crate::tool::ToolArgs;` if not already imported, (2) add `ToolArgs` to the derive attribute list, (3) add `Serialize` to the derive list if not already present (ToolArgs requires Serialize), (4) replace the manual `fn input_schema(&self) -> Value` body with `XxxArgs::json_schema()`, (5) ensure doc comments on struct fields match the description text in the existing manual schema. Tools needing ToolArgs: ReadTool (read.rs), WriteTool (write.rs), SearchTool (search/mod.rs), LspTool (lsp/tool.rs), TaskTool (task/tool.rs), ToolSearchTool (tool_search.rs), ActionLogTool (action_log.rs), WebFetchTool (web/fetch.rs), WebSearchTool (web/search.rs), SpawnAgentTool (agent/spawn.rs), ForkTool (agent/fork_tool.rs), SignalAgentTool (agent/coord/signal.rs), CloseAgentTool (agent/coord/close.rs). Tools that already have ToolArgs and need NO changes for R1: BashTool (bash.rs), EditTool (edit.rs), ApplyPatchTool (patch.rs).
acceptance: All 16 standard tools have #[derive(ToolArgs)] on their input args structs
All 16 standard tools use XxxArgs::json_schema() for input_schema()
No tool has a manual serde_json::json!({}) schema implementation
Doc comments on struct fields provide accurate schema descriptions
cargo check --workspace passes

requirements.title: Follow-up wiring for filesystem and shell tools (read, write, edit, apply_patch, bash)
requirements.spec: For each tool, THE SYSTEM SHALL add a tool_follow_ups! invocation that registers output-to-follow-on action mappings per docs/design/norn-tools/tool-overview.md. The follow_ups method SHALL be added to the Tool impl. Each follow-up action SHALL have: a name matching the output type (e.g. 'file_content', 'file_not_found'), a description with {field} interpolation where appropriate, a when condition checking output.content fields, an expiry (FileModified for file-modifying tools, TurnScoped or Never for read-only), and optional overrides. Filesystem follow-on mappings: read has 6 output types (file_content, file_not_found, file_empty, file_is_directory, file_too_large_no_range, binary_file), write has 3 (file_written, write_failed_permission_denied, write_failed_directory_not_found, write_blocked_by_convention), edit has 4 (edit_applied, edit_failed_string_not_found, edit_failed_multiple_matches, edit_failed_file_not_found, edit_blocked_by_convention), apply_patch has 5 (patch_applied, patch_failed_context_mismatch, patch_failed_file_not_found, patch_failed_permission_denied, patch_partial_applied), bash has 4 (command_success, command_failed, command_timeout, command_blocked).
acceptance: ReadTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 6 output types from tool-overview.md
WriteTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all output types
EditTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all output types
ApplyPatchTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all output types
BashTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all output types
Each follow-up when condition accurately tests the output.content JSON structure
File-modifying tool follow-ups use FileModified expiry where the file path and hash are available

requirements.title: Follow-up wiring for search, development, and web tools (search, lsp, web_search, web_fetch, tool_search, action_log)
requirements.spec: For each tool, THE SYSTEM SHALL add a tool_follow_ups! invocation with output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md. Search follow-ups: search has 5 output types (matches_found, no_matches, too_many_matches, invalid_pattern, ast_query_no_language). LSP follow-ups: lsp has 7 output types (definitions_found, references_found, hover_info, diagnostics_found, completions_found, symbol_not_found, lsp_unavailable). Web follow-ups: web_search has 3 (results_found, no_results, search_error), web_fetch has 4 (page_content, fetch_failed_network, fetch_failed_http_error, fetch_failed_invalid_url). Discovery follow-ups: tool_search has 2 (tools_found, no_matches), action_log has 3 (entries_found, no_entries, entry_detail).
acceptance: SearchTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 5 output types
LspTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 7 output types
WebSearchTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 3 output types
WebFetchTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 4 output types
ToolSearchTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 2 output types
ActionLogTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 3 output types

requirements.title: Follow-up wiring for agent coordination and task tools (spawn_agent, fork, signal_agent, close_agent, task)
requirements.spec: For each tool, THE SYSTEM SHALL add a tool_follow_ups! invocation with output-to-follow-on mappings per tool-overview.md. Agent follow-ups: fork has 2 (fork_created, fork_failed), spawn_agent has 2 (agent_spawned, spawn_failed), signal_agent has 3 (signal_delivered, signal_failed_agent_not_found, signal_failed_channel_closed), close_agent has 2 (agent_closed, agent_not_found). Task follow-ups: task has 5 (task_created, task_updated, task_list, task_not_found, task_dependency_cycle).
acceptance: ForkTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 2 output types
SpawnAgentTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 2 output types
SignalAgentTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 3 output types
CloseAgentTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 2 output types
TaskTool has tool_follow_ups! covering all 5 output types
Agent tool follow-ups use TurnScoped expiry keyed on the spawned agent_id

requirements.title: Schema parity verification
requirements.spec: For every tool where ToolArgs replaces a manual schema, THE SYSTEM SHALL verify that the generated schema is semantically equivalent to the old manual schema. Specifically: all required fields remain required, all optional fields remain optional, types match (string, integer, boolean, enum, array), description text is present and accurate. WHEN the ToolArgs-generated schema differs from the manual schema in a meaningful way (missing field, wrong type, lost required constraint), THE SYSTEM SHALL adjust the struct definition or doc comments to produce the correct schema. Cosmetic differences (key ordering, whitespace) are acceptable.
acceptance: For each tool, a test asserts that XxxArgs::json_schema() produces a schema with the same required fields, property types, and enum values as the old manual schema
Tests are in a new module crates/norn/src/tools/schema_parity_tests.rs
All 16 tools pass schema parity checks

requirements.title: Clippy, tests, and line count compliance
requirements.spec: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings SHALL pass clean. All existing tests SHALL pass without modification. No file SHALL exceed 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace). WHEN adding tool_follow_ups! pushes a file over 500 lines, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract the follow-up definitions into a separate submodule (e.g. read_follow_ups.rs).
acceptance: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0
cargo test --workspace passes all existing and new tests
tokei reports all modified files under 500 code lines
No #[allow(...)] or #[expect(...)] directives added

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the ToolArgs derive macro implementation in norn-macros
SHALL NOT modify the tool_follow_ups! macro implementation in norn-macros
SHALL NOT modify the Tool trait
SHALL NOT change tool behavior — only schema generation and follow-up registration
SHALL NOT add new tools or remove existing tools from the registry
SHALL NOT modify register_standard_tools beyond what schema/follow-up changes require
verification: All 16 standard tools have #[derive(ToolArgs)] on their input args structs
All 16 standard tools have tool_follow_ups! wired with output mappings
Schema parity tests pass for all 16 tools
